description_long: This object is a rectangular section of a stone wall, constructed from irregularly shaped, dark gray stones that are tightly fitted together. The stones have a rough, uneven surface, with visible highlights and shadows that accentuate their three-dimensional texture. The wall features protruding stone ends on both sides, suggesting an interlocking or modular design for structural stability. The top and bottom edges are capped with a single row of larger, flatter stones, providing a defined boundary. The coloration is predominantly dark gray with subtle variations, giving the appearance of weathered stone. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering on the surface.
description: A dark gray, rough-textured stone wall section with interlocking ends and capped top and bottom edges.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the stone wall, with protruding stone ends and a clear view of the stone texture.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the wall and the staggered, protruding stone ends.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the stone pattern and capped edges, but with less pronounced highlights.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness and the protruding stone ends.
attribute_location_context: urban retaining wall or boundary in a city park or along a sidewalk
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering or erosion; the stones appear clean and well-maintained.
